title: Vision & Mission
headings: Essence of the La Montessori Way of Learning
breadcrumb: Home › Vision & Mission
body: ← Back to Home Vision & Mission Vision "Groom each Child's Potential to Excellence" Mission The School's mission is to holistically develop individuals using Child Centric & Life-Skill based education. Also to prepare Students for Challenges of the future. Essence of the La Montessori Way of Learning Montessorians believe in allowing the child to develop according to their inner laws of development. There are four distinct planes of human growth: 1st Plane – Montessori Wing (MW) Child has a very absorbent mind. LMS works on constructivism and independence using tools of a Joyful way of Learning. 2nd Plane – Primary Wing (PW) Child is in the process of intellectual development. LMS empowers students with foundation laying and inculcates in them a Moral & Social sense. 3rd Plane – Elementary & Senior Wing (EW & SW) This is the most eventful time. LMS ensures that the students can utilise their creativity and develop holistically. This moulds a child into the adult self. 4th Plane – After Schooling Schooling at LMS leads a person to be competent, self disciplined and socially well adjusted. The Montessori Education System is characterized by an emphasis on independence, freedom within limits and respect for a child's natural psychological development as well as technological advancement in society. We believe in a constructivist model for learning rather than instructivist. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Examination Process
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process
body: ← Academics & Examination Office Order Ref.: HES/MD(Op.)/UK/OO/07/25 Date: 9th July 2025 Subject: Examination Process Examination Process This manual codifies the complete examination workflow for La Montessori Schools. It prescribes mandatory question-paper formatting, submission routes and timelines; defines a multi-layer review, printing, storage and distribution chain; and sets clear responsibilities for teachers, IT staff, coordinators, administrators and principals. Penalties are detailed for late or erroneous work, from drafting to super-checking and mark entry. 📐 Formatting Guidelines Font, size, and margin standards for question papers — with fine schedule 📬 Submission & Correction Process 40-day prior submission rule, multi-stage review chain, and daily exam timeline 🖨️ Printing Schedule System Coordinator responsibilities and secure storage procedures 🏫 Examination Day Process Timings, correction protocol, super-checking, and rectification flow ⏱️ QP Checking Time Days permitted for checking based on student count (1–35 = 1 day; 36–70 = 2 days) 📋 Rectification Sheet Standard template and field explanations for recording mark changes 💸 Fine Norms Complete penalty schedule for late submission, formatting errors, and correction delays 👁️ Invigilation Duty Minimum 2.5 hours/day; rules for reduced invigilation during checking window 💻 CSM Updation All data to update by 3 PM before first exam day — attendance, marks, remarks 📄 Report Card Printing IT Teacher → System Coordinator → Admin Manager distribution chain LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: CSM Updation
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › CSM Updation
body: ← Examination Process CSM Updation The CSM (Central School Management) portal must be fully updated before the first examination day of each term. The IT Teacher is responsible for coordinating this process. Deadline All CSM data must be updated and verified by 3:00 PM on the working day before the first examination . What Must Be Updated # Data Item Notes 1 Attendance All attendance records up to date for the term 2 Height & Weight Current measurements for all students 3 Subject Marks — CWA Class Work Assessment marks for all subjects 4 Subject Marks — RT Regular Test marks for all subjects 5 Co-Scholastic Marks Grades for co-curricular and life-skills areas 6 Student Remarks Teacher remarks for the term, reviewed and approved by the Principal Report Card Generation After the last examination of the term, report cards must be generated and ready by the last working day of the term . See Report Card Printing for the full printing and distribution process. The IT Teacher must cross-check CSM data against the physical registers maintained at each branch before the deadline. Any discrepancy must be escalated to the Principal on the same day. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Examination Day Process
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › Examination Day Process
body: ← Examination Process Examination Day Process Timings, protocols, and responsibilities for invigilators, teachers, and the System Coordinator on each day of the examination. Key Timings Time Activity 8:45 AM Question papers distributed to invigilators; seating verified 9:00 AM Examination begins; question papers handed to students 9:30 AM No student allowed entry after this time; late arrivals forfeit the examination Post-Examination Protocol Step Process Responsible 1 Correction: Answer sheets checked by the subject teacher of the branch where the student studies Subject Teacher 2 Counter-School Exchange: Answer sheets are exchanged between counter schools for independent verification System Coordinator 3 Super-Checking: A senior teacher reviews marks using a black pen only. Any change must be initialled HOD / Senior Teacher 4 Rectification Sheet: A Rectification Sheet is filled for every mark change identified during super-checking Super-Checking Teacher 5 Virtual Meeting: Held if content errors are found in the question paper (error in QP affects all students uniformly) System Coordinator + Principal 6 Final CSM Update: Marks entered into the CSM portal only after the Principal signs off on the Rectification Sheet IT Teacher Super-checking must be done using a black pen exclusively . Any corrections made in a different colour or without initials will not be accepted and must be redone. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Fine Norms
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › Fine Norms
body: ← Examination Process Fine Norms Fines are levied for violations at various stages of the examination process. All fines are deducted directly from salary. Question Paper Submission Fines Violation Fine Late submission of QP (per day, per paper) Rs. 50 / day / paper QP not submitted by the examination date Rs. 1,000 + Rs. 1,000 (i.e., Rs. 2,000 total) Formatting Fines Violation Fine Incorrect font or font size Rs. 50 per paper Incorrect margin settings Rs. 50 per paper Other formatting mistakes in question paper Rs. 5 per mistake Answer Sheet & Correction Fines Violation Fine Late return of answer sheets (per day) Rs. 50 / day Marking mistake found during super-checking Rs. 1 per mistake Fines accumulate. A teacher who submits a QP 5 days late with 3 formatting errors would incur: Rs. 250 (late) + Rs. 15 (formatting) = Rs. 265 . All fines are tracked by the System Coordinator and reported to the Admin Manager for salary processing. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Formatting Guidelines
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › Formatting Guidelines
body: ← Examination Process Formatting Guidelines All question papers must strictly follow the formatting specifications below. Non-compliance attracts fines as listed. Font Specifications by Subject Subject Font Size English Bookman Old Style 11 pt Hindi KrutiDev10 14 pt Sanskrit KrutiDev10 14 pt Mathematics Times New Roman 12 pt Computer Bookman Old Style 11 pt All other subjects Bookman Old Style 11 pt Margins All four margins (Top, Bottom, Left, Right) must be set to 0.63 inches for every question paper. Fine Structure for Formatting Errors Violation Fine Incorrect font or font size Rs. 50 per paper Incorrect margin settings Rs. 50 per paper Other formatting mistakes in QP Rs. 5 per mistake Formatting errors discovered after printing will still attract the full fine. Teachers are responsible for proofreading their question papers before submission to the System Coordinator. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Invigilation Duty
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › Invigilation Duty
body: ← Examination Process Invigilation Duty All teachers are required to perform invigilation duty during examination periods. The rules below govern minimum hours and special exemptions. Minimum Invigilation Requirement Every teacher must invigilate for a minimum of 2.5 hours per examination day . Reduced Invigilation Allowance Teachers whose own subject examinations concluded in the past 2 days may be assigned a reduced invigilation window, at the Principal's discretion, to allow sufficient time for answer sheet checking. This exception applies only when the teacher's answer-sheet checking workload directly conflicts with invigilation duty. The Principal must record this in the duty register and inform the System Coordinator. The teacher must still be available on campus for the full school day. Invigilation Responsibilities Invigilators must be in the examination hall before 8:45 AM to verify seating arrangements. No student is permitted entry after 9:30 AM ; the invigilator must enforce this strictly. Mobile phones are strictly prohibited inside the examination hall for both students and invigilators. Invigilators must maintain a strict silence environment and circulate continuously during the examination. Any instance of malpractice must be immediately reported to the Principal and documented. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Printing Schedule
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › Printing Schedule
body: ← Examination Process Printing Schedule The System Coordinator is responsible for coordinating the printing, distribution, and secure storage of all question papers across LMCS branches. Responsibilities The System Coordinator manages the complete printing schedule for each examination cycle. The System Coordinator must share the final printing schedule with the Admin Manager at least one week prior to the examination start date. Printed question papers must be stored in a secure room with restricted access until the day of the examination. Access to printed question papers before the examination is strictly limited to the System Coordinator and, where necessary, the Admin Manager. Any breach of question paper confidentiality will be treated as a serious misconduct matter and referred to the MD Operations. Distribution Protocol Question papers are distributed to individual branches on the morning of the examination or as per the pre-approved printing schedule. Branch Principals sign the receipt for question papers received from the System Coordinator. Any shortfall or damage to printed papers must be reported immediately to the System Coordinator. Confidentiality of question papers is non-negotiable. Any leakage — whether intentional or due to negligence — will result in disciplinary action including possible termination. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: QP Checking Time
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › QP Checking Time
body: ← Examination Process QP Checking Time The number of days permitted for checking answer sheets is determined by the number of students whose papers a teacher must correct. This ensures fair timelines across branches of different sizes. Checking Time Allocation Number of Students Days Allowed for Checking 1 – 35 students 1 day 36 – 70 students 2 days Important Notes The checking period begins the day after the examination for that subject. Answer sheets not returned within the permitted window will attract fines as per the Fine Norms . Teachers must complete checking, totalling, and initialling within the allocated period — super-checking by the HOD happens after this. If a teacher is absent during the checking window, the Principal must arrange an alternate checker immediately and report the situation to the System Coordinator. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Rectification Sheet
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › Rectification Sheet
body: ← Examination Process Rectification Sheet A Rectification Sheet must be filled for every mark change identified during super-checking. This document serves as the official record of all mark corrections and must be signed by the Principal before any update is made in the CSM portal. Standard Template Branch Subject Date of Exam Checked By Super-Checked By S.No. Roll No. Class Student Name Old Marks New Marks Total Increased Reason for Change Rows filled individually for each mark change Field Explanations Field Description Branch Name of the LMCS branch (e.g., LMS 1, LMS 3) Subject Name of the subject whose answer sheet is being rectified Date of Exam Date on which the examination was conducted Checked By Name and signature of the teacher who performed the initial marking Super-Checked By Name and signature of the HOD or senior teacher who performed super-checking Old Marks The marks as originally awarded by the checking teacher New Marks The revised marks after super-checking Total Increased Difference between new marks and old marks (positive value only; decreases are also recorded but noted separately) Reason for Change Brief explanation: e.g., "Marking error — Question 3b answer correct but marked wrong", "Totalling error", "Missed sub-question" Mandatory: No CSM portal update may be made without the Principal's signature on the Rectification Sheet. The IT Teacher must retain a copy of every signed Rectification Sheet for audit purposes. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Report Card Printing
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › Report Card Printing
body: ← Examination Process Report Card Printing Report cards are generated from the CSM portal and distributed to branches through a defined chain of responsibility. Printing & Distribution Chain Step Responsible Action 1 IT Teacher Generates PDF report cards from the CSM portal for all students, branch-wise. Verifies data accuracy against submitted marks and remarks. 2 System Coordinator Reviews the generated PDFs for completeness and formatting. Approves batch for printing and coordinates the physical printing process. 3 Admin Manager Receives printed report cards branch-wise. Coordinates distribution to each branch Principal for delivery to parents. 4 Branch Principal Distributes report cards to class teachers, who hand them to parents/guardians on the designated report card day. Deadline Report cards must be printed and ready for distribution by the last working day of the term . Important Rules Report cards must not be handed to students directly — they must always go to a parent or authorised guardian. If a parent has an outstanding fee dues, the report card may be withheld at the Principal's discretion pending clearance. Digital copies (PDF) may be shared via the school communication system only with written permission from the MD Operations. Any error found after printing must be corrected via a Rectification Sheet and re-printed at the earliest — the incorrect version must be retrieved and destroyed. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Submission & Correction Process
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Academics & Examination › Examination Process › Submission & Correction Process
body: ← Examination Process Submission & Correction Process Question papers must be submitted well in advance and go through a structured multi-stage review chain before they are approved for printing. Submission Deadline Question papers must be submitted to the HOD at least 40 days prior to the first day of the examination. Late submissions attract fines as per the Fine Norms . Review Chain Stage Responsible Person Action 1 Subject Teacher Prepares question paper and submits to HOD 2 HOD / Senior Subject Teacher Reviews for content accuracy and blueprint compliance 3 Subject Teacher Incorporates HOD feedback and resubmits 4 IT Teacher Checks formatting (fonts, margins, layout) 5 System Coordinator Final review, approval, and printing coordination Daily Timeline During Examination The following timeline governs each examination day from correction to final mark upload: Activity Timeline Answer sheets distributed to respective teachers for checking Same day after exam Teachers complete checking (see QP Checking Time ) Within 1–2 days Super-checking by HOD / senior teacher using black pen After initial checking Rectification Sheet filled for any mark changes During super-checking Virtual meeting (if content errors found) As required Final marks updated in CSM portal after Principal approval After rectification sign-off No mark may be updated in the CSM portal without the Principal's written approval on the Rectification Sheet. Unauthorised mark changes are treated as a serious misconduct. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Appointments & HR › Fines
body: ← Appointments & HR Fines Violation Fine Late submission of Question Paper & Solution Rs. 50 / day / paper Teacher does not submit QP & Solution till Exam Rs. 1,000 per QP + Rs. 1,000 per Solution = Rs. 2,000 per paper (in addition to the late submission fine) QP and Solution not in separate files Rs. 50 / paper Formatting error (font style, size, bold, heading, line spacing) Rs. 50 / paper; Rs. 5 for minor errors Mistakes in Question Paper Rs. 5 / mistake Late submission of Answer Sheet Rs. 50 / day / paper Super-Checking errors Rs. 1 / mistake Refusal to check allocated Question Papers ½ × (No. of Unchecked Papers ÷ Total Allocated Papers) × Current CTI Example: 10 unchecked of 100 allocated, CTI = Rs. 15,000 → Fine = Rs. 750 LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Process of Hiring
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Appointments & HR › Process of Hiring
body: ← Appointments & HR Process of Hiring Before Interview An Employee applies either from the Website, Facebook or in person, at the Office. The entry of such an applicant must be entered into the School Software along with the number on the Application Form. All of the Applicants must be called for Interview together. Interview The following areas must be assessed during the interview: Spouse's Occupation — Scope of transfer, monetary needs, job stability. Family Setup — Nuclear vs. joint family for support structure; children's age and school. Residence — Ease of reaching School. Academic Qualification — Type of school attended; subject background and combination; Marks in Class 10 & 12; Private vs. Regular degree; Open Schooling; gaps between years; CTET / TET / D.El.Ed / B.Ed. Professional Skills — Computer literacy: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, General. Experience — Duration, periods taken, school timing, subjects taught, salary drawn; extra hobbies; dance/music for Annual Function. Commitment to Serve — Reasons for applying; marital/family status. Minimum Monthly Salary — Ask for the lowest acceptable salary; evaluate candidates quoting high by cross-checking qualifications. Overall Attributes — Confidence, English proficiency, strengths & weaknesses; Demo class if required; student feedback. Subject to performance in the Interview, call for a Demo Class. Demo Class Observe the class for: Class Discipline Content Teaching Methodology Creativity in Teaching Half of the period must be spent observing the teacher through the CCTV cameras for Class Discipline & Changes in Attitude. Subject to satisfactory performance, the Employee could be hired. Hiring The Hiring Form needs to be filled and forwarded to the Head Office along with the Bio-Data Form within 1 week of hiring . The Salary on the Hiring Form should be filled as per the dashboard given to each Principal. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Resignation Rules
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Appointments & HR › Resignation Rules
body: ← Appointments & HR Resignation Rules Before accepting an employee's resignation from the post held, the following must be verified: The resignation is not submitted in the middle of the academic session, as prescribed by the affiliating board. Written notice (email or application) regarding the resignation has been submitted to the Head of the Institution. The resignation must be submitted before the 10th of the month ; otherwise, it will be considered for the following month. For an employee who has served for more than 11 months at the institution, three working months' notice (90 days) or salary equivalent to the notice period must be paid. For an employee who has worked for less than 12 months at the institution, one working month's notice (30 days) or salary equivalent must be paid. If the resignation is submitted before completion of three years of service, it must be verified whether the employee has been paid salary for the vacation period and, if so, whether the salary drawn for the immediately preceding vacation period is to be refunded. A NOC (No Objection Certificate) has been obtained from the relevant departments/sections (TPR, Resource Books, Student Records, Salary Account, Student Money Accounts, and any other) of the Institution. If the above conditions are fulfilled, the Head of the Institution shall forward the resignation letter to the Governing Body with suitable opinion/remarks for orders/acceptance. The Management has the right to reject the resignation in the interest of the students. After receiving the orders/acceptance, the same shall be communicated to the employee by the Head of the Institution concerned. The salary for the month in which the resignation is submitted, and thereafter, shall not be drawn until the Management accepts the resignation. The Governing Body shall be the competent authority to accept the resignation of all employees. If an employee gives notice of resignation but withdraws it before the end of the notice period , only the date of joining changes; all privileges, perks and salaries remain the same. If an employee resigns and is off service for more than a week , they shall be treated as a fresh recruit and shall restart the probation period. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: RRF & Security
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Appointments & HR › RRF & Security
body: ← Appointments & HR Refundable Reserve Fund (RRF) & Security Refundable Reserve Fund (RRF) The RRF is a deduction from the employee's salary that the School holds as a refundable security fund . Applies to employees who joined on or after 1 January 2020 . The RRF is intended to be equal to 3 months of an employee's CTI . The RRF is deducted over 3 years at diminishing rates: Period Since DOJ RRF Deduction Rate Up to 12 months 12% 13 – 24 months 9% 25 – 36 months 6% After 36 months 0% The employee is expected to apply for a refund of the RRF after being relieved of School duties. The RRF will be refunded within 3 months of the application date. In the event of lapses in adherence to the rules, the School reserves the right to make suitable monetary deductions. However, any remaining balance shall still be payable. If any dues are pending at the time of leaving, the School will deduct these from the RRF. RRF as a Loan Employees may borrow up to 70% of the total accumulated RRF as an interest-free short-term loan. The borrowed amount must be repaid before the end of each financial year. An employee may take an employee loan a maximum of once every 2 years . The loan may be repaid in instalments deducted from the employee's monthly salary. Security Deposit The Security is an amount deducted from the salary of an employee, retained by the School in case of non-compliance with resignation rules. The balance due at the time of resignation is refundable subject to complete adherence to all resignation rules and applicable deductions. The employee may apply for refund after relieving from duties; the Security will be refunded within 3 months from the date of application. Discontinued for employees joining after 31 December 2019. Employees who joined before this date will be part of the RRF Scheme. In case of any pending dues at the time of leaving, the School shall deduct the same from the Security Fund. The balance shall still be payable. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Inventory & Infrastructure
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Inventory & Infrastructure
body: ← Employee Code of Conduct Inventory & Infrastructure Use of School Infrastructure & Conveyance The employees of the School are allowed to use the Infrastructure of the School for the overall benefit of the School . The priority for the right of usage shall be exercised by the Students first. Employees are personally responsible for the maintenance and upkeep of the School Infrastructure. Employees are also allowed to use the School conveyance facility in case there are vacant seats . The first priority for the right of access to School Conveyance shall be exercised by the students. In case the School Conveyance is fully occupied by the students, the employee shall be expected to make arrangements for their own travel . LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Code of Conduct
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › School Rules › Code of Conduct
body: ← School Rules Employee Code of Conduct Core Professional Commitment Every teacher at La Montessori Schools shall: Place the child's growth, dignity, safety, and learning at the centre of all professional decisions. Support the holistic development of each student — academic, emotional, social, moral, physical and life-skill development. Contribute to a culture of excellence, discipline, care and continuous improvement consistent with the institution's stated vision. Prepare students not only for examinations, but also for the challenges of the future through values, confidence, communication and practical competence. Conduct with Students Teachers shall: Treat every student with respect, patience, fairness and compassion, without discrimination on the basis of gender, religion, caste, language, ability, family background or academic performance. Maintain a child-friendly, safe and encouraging classroom environment that promotes curiosity, participation and confidence. Use positive discipline at all times. Humiliation, intimidation, sarcasm, abusive language, physical punishment or emotional harassment are strictly prohibited . Protect student confidentiality and handle personal, academic and behavioural information responsibly. Be attentive to student wellbeing and promptly report concerns relating to safety, abuse, neglect, bullying or mental distress through school-approved channels. Encourage student participation in co-curricular, sports, cultural, assembly and community-oriented activities. Teaching and Learning Standards Teachers shall: Plan and deliver teaching that is child-centric, inclusive, engaging and age-appropriate, in line with the school's educational philosophy. Integrate life skills, values, communication, collaboration and problem-solving into classroom practice wherever possible. Maintain high academic expectations while providing support for varied learning needs. Assess students honestly, fairly and transparently, and avoid favouritism or manipulation of marks, records or feedback. Keep classroom records, lesson plans, student work and assessment data accurate and up to date. Continuously improve subject knowledge, pedagogy, digital competence and classroom practice through reflection, training and collaboration. Professional Behaviour Teachers shall: Demonstrate punctuality, preparedness and regular attendance in all school duties including classes, assemblies, meetings, examinations, supervision, events and parent interactions. Dress and behave in a manner that reflects professionalism, modesty, self-respect and the reputation of La Montessori Schools. Use respectful language and maintain self-control in all interactions, including during conflict or stress. Refrain from gossip, defamation, harassment, bullying, intimidation or conduct that creates division among staff, students or parents. Avoid any behaviour, online or offline, that may damage the reputation, trust or educational environment of the school. Not en

title: Communication at LMS
headings: TPRs, Circular Registers & Other Reporting Mediums
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › School Rules › Communication at LMS
body: ← School Rules Communication at LMS Verbal / Phone Communication This form of communication should be avoided for official purposes . It is hard to keep track of and can lead to communication gaps and miscommunications. As the easiest form of communication, employees must ensure that any conversation over this mode cannot be quoted as official instruction. TPRs, Circular Registers & Other Reporting Mediums This shall be the most used mode of official communication . Each and every thing of significance shall be mentioned to the immediate superior through this medium. This should also be used to collect and store all forms of data that the employee could need at any given point of time. WhatsApp / SMS / Instant Messaging Can be used for one-sided mass communication and announcements. Must be used for coordination amongst the various employee groups. Can also be used by an employee to escalate beyond the immediate superior when necessary. Particularly useful for out-of-office hours communication. Emails / Official Letters / Applications This form of communication must be used for very important information and formal requests . All emails aimed at coordinators and MDs should be sent to the official school email address. In case of any non-academic communication or formal request, this medium must be used. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Performance Bonus
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Incentives & Bonuses › Performance Bonus
body: ← Incentives & Bonuses Performance Bonus LMCS distributes a bi-annual bonus based on daily reporting scores provided by Principals, combined with other school assessments. Bi-Annual Bonus Structure The overall bonus sum and category ratios are decided by the Governing Body. The overall sum is divided based on employee category: Teachers, Admin Staff, and Helping Staff . Payment Schedule Evaluated Months Bonus Payable With May, June, July, August, September Salaries of November, December, February & March (same year) October, November, December, February, March Salaries of May, June, July & September (next academic year) Bonus Allocation Split ½ of the overall bonus per category is a fixed bonus — equally divided amongst all employees in that category. The remaining ½ is performance-based , calculated on monthly performance scores. Performance Bonus Calculation Follow these steps to calculate the performance bonus for each employee: Sum all individual performance points across all employees in the category. Calculate the multiplier: Divide the total performance bonus pool by the sum of individual points, then round down to the nearest 10 . Multiply each employee's individual score by the multiplier to get their performance bonus amount. Worked Example Employee Performance Score Bonus (Multiplier = Rs. 30) Employee A 55 55 × 30 = Rs. 1,650/- Employee B 62 62 × 30 = Rs. 1,860/- Employee C 67 67 × 30 = Rs. 2,010/- Employee D 74 74 × 30 = Rs. 2,220/- Employee E 71 71 × 30 = Rs. 2,130/- Sum of Points 329 Overall Performance Bonus Pool = Rs. 10,000. Multiplier = 10,000 ÷ 329 = Rs. 30.39 → rounded to Rs. 30. Performance scores are assigned by Principals using the daily reporting format (MCR parameters). Consistent high scores across the evaluated period maximise the performance bonus component. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: System of Increments
headings: Increment Due to Changes in Dearness Allowance (DA)
Changes in Increment Criteria w.e.f. 1st January 2020
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Incentives & Bonuses › System of Increments
body: ← Incentives & Bonuses System of Increments Fixed Annual Increment Salary increments are made with the salary of September every year, payable in October. Increments are made as per the rules and regulations of Government Institutions — typically 3% annual increment . Increment Due to Changes in Dearness Allowance (DA) A change in the percentage of Dearness Allowance may also affect the net pay. DA changes are decided by the School Management based on the financial performance and viability of the schools. DA changes are usually implemented with the first month's salary of the new academic year . Changes in Increment Criteria w.e.f. 1st January 2020 With effect from 1st January 2020 , at the time of joining, each new employee starts with 3 increments already applied to their base salary structure. New employees are subject to no further increments in the total Cost to Institution for the first 3 years from the date of joining. However, employees will experience an annual increase in Net Payable Salary during this period due to the diminishing percentages of the RRF (Refundable Reserve Fund) deduction each year. Increment Eligibility Rule Condition at time of September Increment Increment Applied (Salary Date − Date of Joining) > 36 months Increment + 1 (annual increment granted) (Salary Date − Date of Joining) ≤ 36 months Increment + 0 (no increment this year) The 3-year lock-in applies only to increases in the total Cost to Institution . The employee's take-home pay still increases each year as the RRF deduction percentage reduces (12% → 9% → 6% → 0% after 3 years). LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Fee Clerk cum PRO – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Fee Clerk cum PRO
body: ← Job Roles Fee Clerk cum PRO Usual working timings: 8:45 AM to 5:00 PM . Expected working days per year: 265 to 290 days . Fee Collection Duties Collect fees from all students between prescribed times as per school rules. Generate a report of fees collected on a particular day at the end of the day and present it to the financial head of the institution. Deposit fees collected on a particular day in the bank within 24 hours of collection. Obtain the financial head's signature in the ledger after showing proof of bank transaction via net banking. Check emails for administrative-related school works like NOC, last date for External Activities (EA), affiliation authority. Keep check on all day-to-day money matters: workshops, donations, fines, examination expenses, etc. Log all expenses into the Physical Day Book at the counter and on the Day Book Google Sheet every day. Collect an article from the Editorial Bench of the school after each event and submit it to the press reporter. Maintain all student records (student details, CCE mark updation, etc.) and report to the Principal and concerned authority. Administrative Duties (Working Days) Check emails for administrative-related school work: NOC, EA deadlines, affiliation, CCE mark updation. Maintain student details, parent details and contact information, marks, fees information, and certificates on the school software as well as in hard copy. Maintain teacher records on the software as well as in files. Issue various certificates: Transfer Certificate, Mark List, Migration, "To Whom It May Concern" letters, etc. Cross-check all related information including financial accounts before issuing. Working Days & Holidays One holiday per week — generally Sunday, but may be any day per school requirements. All gazetted holidays of Himachal Pradesh + 2 local holidays are given off. Compensatory leave may be claimed if called on a gazetted holiday. 1 Casual Leave per month (paid). In case of accumulation, leaves may be encashed at the rate of ½ day per accumulated leave. National holidays are expected to be working days. Expected to work for 265 to 290 days per year . LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Games Teacher / PTI – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Games Teacher / PTI
body: ← Job Roles Games Teacher / PTI The PTI must be present in the school campus at least 30 minutes before and after school . School timings: 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM . Stock Maintenance Maintain all sports-related stock and equipment. Keep an up-to-date inventory. Management of Helping Staff Ensure that helping staff perform their prescribed duties. Report irregularities to the Principal. Organise staff for preparation of major events (Investiture, Sports Day, House Competitions, Annual Function, etc.). Allot and administer helping staff before and after official school timing. Timetable Management Make timetable adjustments as per daily circumstances. Be aware of all adjustments of the day and act as a centre for information in this regard. Ensure teachers adhere to the timetable and discipline with the right spirit. During examinations, assign examination duties and ensure smooth conduction by arranging material in advance (sheets, maps, thread, etc.). Money Matters Deal with day-to-day money matters of the school and coordinate with office for Head Office communication. Discipline Act as a right hand to the Principal in managing and coordinating the school, staff, and students. Take disciplinary action against those who violate school norms. Conduct continuous rounds of the school to ensure discipline and cleanliness. Take care of general defaults — be strict on uniform, overall decorum, and English. Counsel students who are sent out by teachers. Physical Education At least 50% of allotted periods must be dedicated to theory for Classes XI and XII. Conduct fortnightly tests of theory taught. Set examination papers as per the school routine. Sports Competitions Liaise with sports authorities for coordination and sports functioning. Prepare sports teams for cluster games. May include sports practices on holidays and early mornings before school. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Helper – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Helper
body: ← Job Roles Helper Reporting time: 7:45 AM Daily Duties Reporting time is 7:45 AM . Open the school premises and do dusting and sweeping/cleaning of the gate area, offices, and other specific areas instructed by the in-charge. After cleaning, be present around the gate area to receive students, listen to parents' issues pertaining to students, and inform the superior if needed. During morning assembly, the primary duty is to help the teachers. Perform prescribed duties in the Montessori Wing during school hours, along with washroom duty. Stamping notebooks during the day is also part of a helper's duty. Helpers are responsible for the safety, well-being, and discipline of students. Helpers must exercise responsibility especially during the dispersal time of the Montessori Wing and Primary Wing. Helpers assigned to school buses must perform bus duty as the conductor of the bus. Helpers must ensure they have lunch at different times so that the majority of helpers are available to teachers and students at all given times. Helpers must clean the Montessori classrooms between 1 PM and 3 PM. Facilitate the dispersal process of the senior students. From 3 PM onwards (after school), sweep and dust all classrooms. Ensure that the water supply is regular (switch on the motor at the right times). Responsible for watering plants regularly. Must adhere to tasks given by the PTI, Fee Clerk, Principal, and Teachers. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: IT Teacher – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › IT Teacher
body: ← Job Roles IT Teacher Marks Updation Check and compile CWA results on a fortnightly basis for parent-principal interaction. Compile FA & SA marks at the end of the respective time period. Assist teachers in the data entry process when help is required. Computer Laboratory & Hardware Maintenance of computer lab — hardware and software. Maintain and troubleshoot the EPABX system installed in the school campus. Look after and provide basic troubleshooting for CCTV cameras and all other hardware and software installed at the school. Disciplinary Duty Keep a check on the fine register and collection. Digital Classes Keep a check on all digital classes to ensure they are working properly. Update the digital class record regularly. Check the peripherals of digital classes and lab for proper working. Generate a Monthly Usage Report and present it at the Monthly Meet. Examination Rate the quality of papers submitted by teachers by email. Fix the formatting of papers before printing them. Help in the printing of examination papers. Social Media Responsibilities Facebook: Regular status updates and at least 10 photographs per day. Instagram: 1 photograph per day. Twitter: Status updates. YouTube: Regular videos of weekly events. Website: Choose the best 20 photographs every week and send for the website; provide regular data updates. Data Organisation Methodical daily storage of data (text, videos, photos, presentations). Compression of files and removal of slack files on a regular basis. General Teaching Responsibility The IT Teacher is responsible for the computer lab and all other IT-related jobs including digital classroom management, examination paper printing, and CBSE online updations. They must be ready to stay after school if required. External Assessment (DA) Set up the lab for DA and classes during morning assembly. Make DA schedule and circulate it in time to ensure smooth conduct. Undertake DA tests, giving a uniform time to all students. Computer Classes Take lab and activity classes after the end of each theoretical chapter. At the end of each semester, computers must be formatted and cleaned using Collin. Computers must be dusted monthly using a blower. At least 25% of periods should be taken as theory classes. Must stay updated with the latest developments in computers. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Peon cum Driver – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Peon cum Driver
body: ← Job Roles Peon cum Driver Usual working timings: 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM . Expected minimum working days per year: 220 days . Bus Duties (8–9 AM, 1–2 PM, 3–4 PM) Reach the bus starting point 5–10 minutes before departure for basic dusting and cleaning of the bus. Inspect the bus for signs of wear and tear. The driver is responsible for the assigned bus and must look after its service, documents, and maintenance. Drive at moderate speeds — no rash driving. Pick up students from allocated stops and patiently wait for students to board or alight. Use polite language with appropriate distance and body language — preferably English — with parents, students, and staff. Fill up the log book for the bus and attend to fuel requirements. Adhere to all norms of driving. In case of an accident, the driver must obtain compensation from the other party involved. Any shortfall is paid by the driver, as accidents are treated as negligence. Duties During School Hours Sit in the assigned corridor / place in the school, ensuring good discipline, and work as a helping hand to teachers and other support staff. Be instrumental in day-to-day jobs at the school: gardening, moving furniture, basic dusting, facilitating games, office running, and other school-related jobs. Must not refuse work assigned by the in-charge (PTI & Principal). For each refusal reported by the in-charge, a half-day's salary will be deducted. Lunch must be taken individually in the bus as per the schedule agreed upon by drivers. Must not use a mobile phone during school hours. Be ready to work extra on special occasions. Duties During Student Holidays Be aware of all documents for their respective vehicle and update them during holidays. Come to school before the beginning of session to help set up the school for re-opening. Must always be on call for school / school management requirements. In case of unavailability, a leave will be deducted. Outstation Duties Scenario Entitlement Outstation duty — no overnight stay Rs. 200/- for the day (all expenses) + bus fare reimbursement on submission of tickets Outstation duty — overnight stay Rs. 500/- per day (all personal expenses incl. food & stay) + bus fare reimbursement on submission of tickets School is closed on a day during the outstation tour 1 Compensatory Leave per off-day at school After completing any outstation tour 1 Compensatory Leave per completed tour Working Days & Holidays One holiday per week — generally Sunday, but may be any day per school requirements. All gazetted holidays of Himachal Pradesh + 2 local holidays are given off. The employee may still be called on a gazetted holiday; compensatory leave may be claimed for up to twice the working time, subject to approval by the head of the institution. Expected to be present for at least one-fourth of the duration of school vacations. The placement of working days within vacations is at the discretion of the head of the institution. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Monte

title: Principal – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Principal
body: ← Job Roles Principal Core Responsibilities Supervise, guide, and control the work of teaching and non-teaching staff of the school. Carry out all administrative duties required of a head of office. Be responsible for the proper maintenance of school records, service records of teachers, and other registers. Oversee money-related handovers to the accounts in-charges. Handle official correspondence and furnish, within specified dates, returns and information required by the State Government / Board. Ensure that fines and misc. charges are realised and appropriately accounted for and duly appropriated for the purpose for which they were levied. Conduct physical verification of school property and stock at the beginning, end of the first semester, and at the end of the year — hence 3 times a year . Make satisfactory arrangements for good drinking water and other facilities for students. Give appropriate suggestions to the Coordinator to ensure that the school building, fixtures, furniture, office equipment, lavatories, playgrounds, and school garden are properly maintained. Be in-charge of admissions, preparation of school timetable, allocation of duties and teaching load to teachers, and provide necessary facilities to teachers in the discharge of their duties. Plan the year's academic work in advance in consultation with colleagues. Hold staff meetings at least once a month, review work done, and assess student progress. Help and guide teachers to promote their professional growth. Actively encourage participation in in-service education. Promote teachers' initiative for self-improvement and encourage educationally sound experiments. Supervise classroom teaching and secure co-operation among teachers of the same subject as well as inter-subject coordination. Arrange for special remedial teaching for children who need it. Plan and specify a regular timetable for scrutiny of pupils' written work and homework. Ensure timely and effective assessment and correction. Organise and coordinate various co-curricular activities through the house system. Send regularly the progress reports of students to their parents or guardians by means of Monthly Open Houses (MOH). Promote the physical well-being of pupils; ensure high standards of cleanliness and health habits. Devote at least 18 periods per week to teaching. title: Teacher – Job Roles
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Job Roles › Teacher
body: ← Job Roles Teacher General Job Roles Act as a friend, guide, mentor, motivator, facilitator, preacher, and role model to students. Teach and facilitate learning among students. Be receptive to queries and clear them to the best of your abilities. Assess student progress through quizzes, tasks, projects, assessments, written and oral tests and assignments. Maintain a softcopy of marks on the School Software and a hardcopy record in the TPR. Dedicate a week or more for revision of the syllabus before each terminal exam. Communicate in the School only in English . Guide children on proper pronunciation and language skills. Maintain attendance for respective classes. Prepare Blueprint, Paper Setting, Marking Scheme and Solutions for examination papers. Undertake examination duty, followed by Checking, Super-Checking and marks updation on the School Management Software. Stay up to date on new trends and changes in the education sector through workshops, seminars, self-research, journals, and websites. Use audio-visual aids like Digital Classrooms to make learning a fun-filled experience. Ensure discipline within school premises. Prepare children to be future model citizens of the country. Educate students on etiquettes, health, and personal hygiene especially during lunch break. Impart student-centric education. Include students in remedial classes, especially under-achievers. Counsel students at various points of schooling. Organise outdoor activities like field trips, picnics, and games with an aim of providing holistic and practical education. Prepare students and fellow teachers for school events like Annual Functions, Investiture Ceremonies, Sports Days, and Morning Assemblies. Take classes (periods) as per the requirement of the school in the best interest of students. Hold various positions of responsibility like house moderator, activity in-charge, etc. Formulate a lesson plan and execute it as per CBSE curriculum while incorporating activities to challenge students' creativity. Maintain a TPR (Teacher's Personal Record) in detail. Update marks on the School Management Software along with a record in the TPR. Work as a good link between students, management, and parents. Maintain cordial relations with parents, fellow faculty, and management. Inform and guide parents about the progress of their children. Language Teachers Give special emphasis to the pronunciation of spoken language. Practise and evaluate Listening, Reading, Writing, and Speaking skills in the classroom. Work on students' writing skills; prepare appropriate content for the School Blog or magazine. (Sanskrit Teacher specific) Ensure correct Mantra Ucharna (chanting) pronunciation. Science Teacher Conduct teaching more by practicals than by textbooks; give hands-on experience alongside text-book teaching. It is recommended that Science is taught without the use of a physical textbook in class. Identify, help, and prepare students for various Science-based competitions and

title: Day Leaves
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Day Leaves
body: ← Leave & Vacation Rules Day Leaves Day-based leave categories cover full-day absences. Each has different approval requirements and salary implications. Single Leave (Unpaid) A Single Leave is one unpaid working day off. It is granted when an employee has exhausted their Casual Leave balance for the year. Treated as an unpaid day — one day's salary is deducted. Requires prior written application and Principal's approval. If the leave is sandwiched around a public holiday (i.e., taken the day before or after a Sunday/gazetted holiday), the holiday is also counted as a Single Leave (sandwich rule). The sandwiched day becomes unpaid as well. Double Leave A Double Leave counts as two leave days for one calendar day's absence. It applies in specific circumstances: Maximum ⌊(total teachers at branch ÷ 10)⌋ Double Leaves may be active simultaneously at a single branch on any given day. If the limit is reached, additional applications will be deferred. Consecutive absences exceeding 6 working days trigger automatic classification as a Long Leave , which requires MD Operations approval and may affect service continuity. Absent An employee is marked "Absent" when they fail to report to duty without prior approval and without notifying the school. Rule Details Leave equivalent 1 Absent = 2 unpaid leave days deducted from salary Notification deadline Employee must notify the Principal by 6:00 PM the previous evening if they cannot attend the next day. Failure to notify = Absent (not Single Leave) Break in service 7 or more consecutive Absent days without communication = break in service . The employee may be treated as having abandoned their post, and disciplinary proceedings will be initiated Employees who are marked Absent repeatedly (more than 3 times in a term) will be counselled by the Principal. A fourth instance in the same term may result in a formal show-cause notice. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: General Leave Rules
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › General Leave Rules
body: ← Leave & Vacation Rules General Leave Rules The following 11 rules apply to all employees across all LMCS branches, regardless of leave category. Leave is a privilege, not a right. All leave must be applied for and approved in advance. Unapproved absence is treated as "Absent" and carries a double-leave deduction. First-come, first-served. When two employees apply for the same date, the earlier application takes precedence. No two staff members in the same role at the same branch may be on leave simultaneously without prior approval from the MD Operations. One-day advance written notice required. Leave applications must be submitted to the Principal at least one working day before the intended leave date. Emergency leaves must be communicated by 6:00 PM the previous day (see Absent rules). Responsibility handover is mandatory. Before proceeding on leave, the employee must hand over all pending duties, registers, and responsibilities to a designated colleague. Failure to do so may result in leave being treated as unauthorised. External remuneration = marked on leave. Any employee who takes on paid work outside LMCS (private tuition, event hosting, freelance assignments, etc.) on a working day will be marked on leave for that day, regardless of physical presence at school. WOH 2-hour absence = half day. An employee absent for 2 or more hours during the Work-On-Hours (WOH) period — without prior approval — is marked as a half-day leave. Sanctioned leave does not guarantee pay. Whether leave is paid depends on the leave category (CL = paid; Unpaid Leave = unpaid). The employee must confirm the leave type with the Admin Manager before proceeding. Leave lapses on resignation. Any unused Casual Leave balance lapses on the date of resignation or termination. There is no encashment of CL at the time of leaving service. Leave during the first 3 months of service. Employees in their probationary period (first 3 months) are not entitled to Casual Leave. Any absence during this period is treated as Unpaid Leave. Sandwiched leaves. If a leave is taken on a day immediately before or after a holiday (Sunday, gazetted holiday, or school vacation), the intervening holiday is also counted as leave unless specifically exempted by the MD Operations. Leave records are maintained centrally. The Admin Manager maintains the master leave register. All branch Principals must report leaves by the end of each working day to ensure accurate records. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Leave Benefits
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Leave Benefits
body: ← Leave & Vacation Rules Leave Benefits LMCS provides two categories of paid leave benefits: Casual Leave (CL) and Compensatory Leave. Both are governed by strict eligibility and usage rules. Casual Leave (CL) Rule Details Entitlement — Teaching Staff 1 Casual Leave per month (12 CL per academic year) Entitlement — Admin / Helping Staff ½ of every vacation day is counted as CL (see Non-Teaching Staff Vacations ) Nature Paid leave — salary is not deducted when CL is used Claim Deadline Must be claimed by 31 March of the academic year; unused CL lapses after this date Carry-Forward Not permitted. CL does not carry forward to the next academic year On Resignation / Termination Lapses immediately; no encashment of unused CL Half-Day CL A half-day leave taken against CL is counted as one full CL deducted from the balance Compensatory Leave Rule Details Eligibility Granted when an employee is required to work on a holiday or vacation day at the school's request Nature Paid leave — equivalent in value to CL Approval Chain Recommended by the Branch Principal → approved by MD Operations . Compensatory Leave is not valid without MD Operations approval. Encashment Not permitted. Compensatory Leave cannot be converted to cash Claim Deadline Must be claimed by 31 March of the same academic year; unused Compensatory Leave lapses after this date Employees are advised to track their CL balance monthly and plan leave accordingly. The Admin Manager publishes individual leave balance statements at the start of each term. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Non-Teaching Staff Vacations
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Non-Teaching Staff Vacations
body: ← Leave & Vacation Rules Office Order Ref.: OO/05/21 Date: 4th February 2021 Subject: Vacation Period — Leave Treatment for Non-Teaching Staff Non-Teaching Staff Vacations Administrative staff, helping staff, fee clerks, and other non-teaching employees are expected to work during periods when teaching staff are on vacation. This office order defines how their Casual Leave accrues during such periods. The Rule For every 2 working days that a non-teaching employee works during a vacation period, they accrue 1 Casual Leave (CL) . In other words: ½ of the working days in the vacation period = CL granted . Worked Example Consider a vacation period of 26 calendar days : Item Count Total vacation calendar days 26 Less: Sundays and Saturdays (off days) −5 Less: Gazetted holidays within the period −2 Working days (non-teaching staff report) 19 CL accrued (½ of 19, rounded down) 9.5 CL Important Notes This accrual is in addition to the standard 1 CL/month entitlement for non-teaching staff. CL accrued under this order is subject to the same claim deadline (31 March) and no-carry-forward rules as regular CL. Non-teaching staff who are absent during a vacation working day will have that day deducted from their accrued CL for that period. The Admin Manager calculates and communicates the CL credit to each non-teaching employee at the end of each vacation period. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Special & Work-Based Leave
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Special & Work-Based Leave
body: ← Leave & Vacation Rules Special & Work-Based Leave These leave categories are tied to school events, non-compliance, or special circumstances rather than a simple day-off request. Special Day Leave A Special Day Leave counts as 2 leave days and is applied when an employee is absent on any of the following high-priority school days: First or last day of an academic term Annual Function day Investiture Ceremony Sports Day External events where school representation is required Combined meetings called by the Principal or Head Office MD Operations meetings / all-staff briefings Absence on any of the above days without prior written approval from the MD Operations is automatically recorded as a Special Day Leave (2 leave days deducted). Special Day Absent If an employee is absent on a Special Day and fails to inform the school by the previous evening's deadline, the deduction escalates to 5 leave days . Scenario Deduction Absent on a Special Day (notified in advance) 2 leave days (Special Day Leave) Absent on a Special Day (no prior notification) 5 leave days (Special Day Absent) Non-Compliance Leave Non-Compliance Leave is deducted when an employee fails to complete an assigned responsibility, submit a required document, or follow a school process by its deadline — even if the employee was physically present at school. Rule Details Deduction per instance −0.1 leave per non-compliance instance Conversion 10 non-compliance instances = 1 full leave day deducted Frequency Calculated and deducted monthly from the employee's leave balance Examples of non-compliance Missing QP submission deadline, not updating CSM data on time, failing to sign registers, not handing over duties before leave Non-Compliance deductions are automatically flagged by the System Coordinator and forwarded to the Admin Manager. Employees receive a monthly summary of any deductions recorded against them. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Time-Based Leaves
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Service Rules & Leaves › Leave & Vacation Rules › Time-Based Leaves
body: ← Leave & Vacation Rules Time-Based Leaves Time-based leaves accumulate as fractional leave days based on the duration of partial absence. When sufficient instances accumulate, they convert into full leave deductions. Late Leave Rule Details Definition Arriving after the official reporting time Grace period Within 5 minutes of the official reporting time — not counted as Late Leave Conversion rate 4 Late Leaves = 1 full leave day deducted Recording Recorded in the branch register by the Principal and reported to the Admin Manager weekly Short Leave Rule Details Definition Leaving early or being absent for a part of the working day (less than a half day) Conversion rate 3 Short Leaves = 1 full leave day deducted Calculation Proportional to the work-day hours — each hour of absence within the workday is tracked. When 3 such instances are recorded, one full leave is deducted. Half Leave Rule Details Definition Absence covering approximately half the working day (typically the first half or second half of the school day) Conversion rate 2 Half Leaves = 1 full leave day deducted Minimum hours requirement The employee must have worked at least the minimum required hours on the day in question for it to qualify as a Half Leave rather than an Absent Summary Table Leave Type Instances to = 1 Full Leave Grace / Threshold Late Leave 4 Within 5 minutes = no Late Leave recorded Short Leave 3 Per-hour tracking; must be < half-day absence Half Leave 2 Must have worked minimum required hours LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Outstation Driver Rules
breadcrumb: Home › Employee Code of Conduct › Transport & Logistics › Outstation Driver Rules
body: ← Transport & Logistics Office Order Ref.: HES/MD(Op.)/UK/OO/02/18 Date: 3rd October 2018 Subject: Outstation Driver Rules Outstation Driver Rules It has been decided by the Management that the following will be the terms and conditions for outstation drivers. Outstation Duty is one that does not include a visit to one of the Schools. Day Trip (No Overnight Stay) The driver will be entitled to Rs. 200/- for the day for all sorts of expenses. The driver will also be entitled to claim fare of bus travel, subject to the submission of Bus Tickets. Overnight Outstation Duty The driver is entitled to Rs. 500/- per day for all personal expenses including food etc. If the tour includes bus travel, the driver will be entitled to claim the fare, subject to submission of Bus Tickets. In a case where a driver is on outstation duty and the School of the driver is closed (Off-Day), the driver is entitled to 1 Compensatory Leave per Off-Day at School. The driver is entitled to 1 Compensatory Leave after each tour . LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Frequently Asked Questions
headings: Can my child celebrate her/his birthday in School?
Is my child allowed to carry money to School?
What do I need to do to update any personal information?
How do I recover my child's lost belongings?
What if my child forgets to carry her/his lunch box?
What is the procedure if my child has to take leave of absence for a few days?
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › FAQ
body: ← Student Code of Conduct Frequently Asked Questions Can my child celebrate her/his birthday in School? Yes, toffees / candies / chocolates costing less than or equal to Rs. 5 are allowed. It is further requested to NOT SEND 'Mithai' / presents, etc. for distribution to the Classmates and the Teachers. Is my child allowed to carry money to School? No. Children are not allowed to carry money to School, with the exception of when it is being asked by the Teacher. What do I need to do to update any personal information? Parents can either request for any type of change in information using the online portal at www.lms.org.in OR send an application to the respective Class Teacher for any kind of change in information viz. address, telephone / mobile numbers, email ID, Adhaar Card Information etc. How do I recover my child's lost belongings? 'Prevention is better than cure'. The name, class and section of the student should be clearly marked on all belongings of the students — e.g. blazers, sweaters, ties, belts, water bottles, tiffins etc. In case your ward has lost her/his belongings, please write to your child's class teacher and get the matter resolved. What if my child forgets to carry her/his lunch box? Don't worry. If informed in time, the Class Teacher ensures that your child will not go hungry . What is the procedure if my child has to take leave of absence for a few days? In case leave is required, the class teacher can be informed through a Leave Application which needs to be duly signed by the authorised parent / guardian. In case of an emergency, the Principal should be informed through phone / SMS / Whatsapp and the leave application must be submitted to the Class Teacher on joining. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Code of Conduct (Students)
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Behaviour & Student Culture › Code of Conduct
body: ← Behaviour & Student Culture Student Code of Conduct Young people who are given the privilege of joining the LMS fraternity should be aware that as members of this distinguished community, they carry the responsibility of upholding its ideals with dignity and pride. As the School's ambassadors, students are expected: To be kind, considerate and upright human beings, always ready to offer encouragement and lend a helping hand. To behave courteously to all, rich or poor, regardless of caste, creed, colour or religion, always respecting the liberty and rights of others. To look upon fellow humans from all parts of the world and all walks of life as brethren, avoiding rudeness and vulgarity, at all times. To make every effort to contribute their share in making the world a better place to live in, rather than idly gossiping, lamenting and grumbling over the evils they perceive. To possess the courage of their convictions, never hesitating to say a firm 'NO' when asked to do a thing, which they know to be wrong, however tempting it might seem. Never to be cruel, knowing that cruelty is the trait of bullies and low self esteem, while kindness is the mark of gentility and strength. To stand up for and personify the spirit of sportsmanship and fair play, never to lie, cheat or be deliberately unfair in order to win. Play the game with full zeal and accept defeat with good grace. To treat staff and visitors in a friendly and respectful manner, at School as well as at home, always rising to wish any teacher or guest politely and offering assistance voluntarily, without the need to be prompted. To accept assigned tasks and duties willingly and without argument, ensuring that these are carried out with the highest standards. To take pride in wearing their School uniform and strive to keep it neat and tidy at all times. To respect and treat public property with care; to look after the School premises with the pride of ownership; to prevent wilful damage whenever possible; and to promptly report any breakages observed. To take good care of themselves and adopt healthy habits, so as to remain mentally and physically active and strong enough to face the most difficult situations with courage and confidence. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Misconduct of Student
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Behaviour & Student Culture › Misconduct of Student
body: ← Behaviour & Student Culture Misconduct of Student The following escalation process applies for student misbehaviour and misconduct: On misbehaviour and misconduct in the Class the Teacher must give three warnings . If the teacher feels that she is unable to control the student, he/she should bring it to the Principal's notice by means of the TPR. After the third mention, the teacher can request the Principal to counsel the student. He/she must also inform the Parent only through a note in the Almanac about the Principal's Counselling. After the third counselling by the Principal, the teacher in consultation with the Principal can request for the Parent to be called for the MOH (Monthly Open House). If the student has not shown any improvement after the parents' third consecutive MOH, the Parent can be called to speak to the Principal. If the student continues to show signs of indiscipline and resistance to improve, the student can be called for a PTA meeting and addressed by the entire PTA Governing committee. In case the student still refuses to show any signs of improvement, the LMS Heads Forum can decide to suspend the student for a maximum of the rest of the Academic Term. In case the Student still refuses to change, the Heads Forum can decide to rusticate the student and the same shall be informed to the PTA Governing Body. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Rewards & Punishments
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Behaviour & Student Culture › Rewards & Punishments
body: ← Behaviour & Student Culture Rewards & Punishments 🔴 Red Star Careless and untidy work, indicating a general lack of effort in academics, may lead to the award of a Red Star by any teacher, which will be recorded on the Star Chart maintained in every classroom. 🔴 Red Card A student who gets 3 Red Stars , indicating a sustained lack of effort in academics, will be liable for the issue of a Red Card. The Head of the School, on the written recommendation of the Class Teacher(s), may issue a Red Card. This will be announced in Assembly and the name of the student will be posted on the School notice board. A student on a Red Card has to have the appropriate column in the card signed by the subject teacher concerned at the end of each period, on each day of the week. If progress during the week is satisfactory, the Head of School may discontinue the Red Card at the end of the week. 🟢 Green Star A Green Star, recorded on the Star Chart, will be awarded to a student for exemplary behaviour and performance within the classroom and the School campus. Any subject teacher can bestow a Green Star. Certificates will be awarded to students accumulating the maximum Green Stars in their respective class. 🟡 Yellow Star Yellow Stars may be given by Teachers for minor infringements of School rules and general misbehaviour. If a student gets 5 Yellow Stars she/he will be issued a Yellow Card. 🟡 Yellow Card Serious offences which adversely reflect a student's conduct — e.g., breaking norms, bullying, rudeness, insubordination, disobedience, cheating, telling lies — may lead to the issue of a Yellow Card. A Yellow Card is a serious matter. The offender is deprived of all privileges in the School for a period of one week. She/he is not allowed to attend any programme, picnic or outing while on a Yellow Card. A student on a Yellow Card must carry the card for the entire week's duration and is liable to have remarks entered on the card for any disciplinary infringement. At the end of the week, the student will meet the Head of the School. The receipt of two Yellow Cards within one academic year will lead to the expulsion of the offender from the School on disciplinary grounds. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: School Rules
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Behaviour & Student Culture › School Rules
body: ← Behaviour & Student Culture School Rules Every student must carry her/his Almanac to School every day and use it productively. Students who do not use school-arranged transport should ensure that they arrive at school at least 10 minutes before the first bell . The warning bell is rung 5 minutes before the start of the morning assembly. Students should move from one classroom to another between periods without wasting time, in an orderly and quiet manner. Students should be habitually clean and always neatly dressed. Non-Sikh boys should get their hair cut at regular intervals and ensure that it remains combed, short and tidy. No shouting or whistling is allowed in or around the School building. Running in corridors is strictly prohibited. It is strictly forbidden to throw any object at any one. Bullying and the use of foul language are punishable offences , whether in or out of School. Care must be taken of all School property and no student should scratch or spoil the desks or chairs, or damage any School furniture, or write or draw anything on the walls. Damage done, even by accident, should be reported at once to the School authorities. The cost of any wilful damage done will be made good by the concerned student, who will also be liable to pay a fine for any such offence. No irrelevant book (other than text books or library books), magazine or other publication may be brought to the School without the Class Teacher's explicit permission. Lending or borrowing of money or other articles is not permitted. Mobile phones, Audio and Video CDs, DVDs, Computer games are strictly banned in the School premises. Bringing these articles to School will lead to confiscation, never to be returned. Students who are escorted to and from School in the care of servants or domestic staff may not leave the School premises without their nominated escort. LMS students should always remember that the School is judged by their conduct outside the School premises , whether in or out of uniform. The School reserves the right to terminate the Schooling of students with unsatisfactory progress in studies or whose conduct is a bad precept for others. Exploding crackers or splashing colours during Diwali/Holi or any other occasion in the school premises or on School buses is strictly forbidden. Students must make every effort to be present for festivals and functions celebrated in the School which they are expected to attend. The School is committed to protect the environment and expects its students to be environment friendly in all their actions. Students should consciously avoid the use of polybags. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document
