title: General Fees Rules
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Fees & Administrative › General Fees Rules
body: ← Fees & Administrative Procedures General Fees Rules Fee Rules — La Montessori Chain of Schools 1.1 Payment Deadline & Late Fee School dues must be paid on or before the 10th of each month . Payment received after the 10th will attract a late fee as follows: Period Late Fee 11th – 15th of the month ₹100 16th – last day of the month ₹200 Every subsequent month of default ₹500 per month 1.2 Fee Counter Timings Day Timings School working days 9:00 am – 1:30 pm & 2:30 pm – 5:00 pm School holidays 10:00 am – 2:00 pm 1.3 Advance Payment & Annual Discount Fees may be deposited in advance for any number of months or for the entire academic year. A 5% discount on tuition fees is applicable to parents who pay the entire yearly tuition fee before 30th April . 1.4 Receipt Policy Important: Fees will be considered paid ONLY upon receipt of an official fee receipt (online or offline). NO RECEIPT = NO FEES PAID. Parents are requested to use the School's Online Fee Portal: https://onlinefee.lms.org.in/ Payment modes that do not generate an instant receipt — such as RTGS, NEFT, IMPS, UPI, cheque, GPay, Paytm, or PhonePe — are discouraged. If used, an immediate confirmation and fee receipt must be obtained from the Fee Office. 1.5 Authorised Payment Personnel Dues must be handed over only to the person at the fee counter . Payments made to any other individual will not be recognised. 1.6 Fee Heads & Transport Fee Tuition fees and conveyance fees are payable for 12 months of the academic year. Transport fee discounts based on days operated: Days Transport Operated Discount 0 – 5 days 50% discount 6 – 10 days 25% discount More than 10 days No discount 1.7 Inter-Branch Migration A migration fee is chargeable at the time of transfer. The fee structure of the destination branch will apply from the date of transfer. If the New Admission Fee paid at the original branch is lower than that of the destination branch, the parent is liable to pay the difference. No refund is applicable if the amount paid is higher. Fees already paid for the current academic year at the original branch will be credited towards the destination branch's fee structure. Any deficit is payable; any surplus is non-refundable. A transfer is considered incomplete until all fees as per the destination branch's fee structure have been cleared. 1.8 Default & Consequences Default Period Consequence More than 3 months Student will be debarred from appearing in examinations More than 6 months Student's name will be struck off from the school roll LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

body: ← Fees & Administrative Procedures New Admission Special Cases 1. Inter LMS Migration of Students A parent can transfer between the Branches of LMS by paying the difference in the New Admission Fee between the Original School Branch and the Destination School Branch. In case the New Admission Fees of the Original LMS Branch has increased, the latest New Admission fee will be considered to calculate the difference. 2. New Parent Referral If "Parent A" refers "Parent B," both will receive a 20% discount on the new admission fees. If "Parent A" refers two or more new admissions, Parent A will get a 40% discount , and each referred parent will get a 20% discount. 2. Old Parent Referral Scheme An old parent is defined as a parent whose student is either active at the School or is an Alumni of the School. If an old parent refers a new admission, the referred new parent can get a 20% discount on the admission fees. The referring old parent will receive an incentive equal to 10% of the collected new admission fees as a discount on their tuition fees. If the referring old parent chooses not to offer the 20% discount to the referred new parent, they will receive 10% of the total admission fees paid by the referred new parent as a discount on their tuition fees. title: Communication with School
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Starting at LMS › Communication with School
body: ← Starting at LMS Communication with School Ways to Communicate There are several ways of communicating with the School: Primary Communication with the Teacher is through the Almanac . Parents can meet the Teacher between 8:45 am and 9:00 am on any School working day. Parents can visit School for Monthly Open House (MOH) as per dates given on a monthly basis by the Teachers. Parents can meet the Principal on Monday, Wednesday & Friday between 10:00 am and 11:00 am on School Working Days. Parents can call the Principal directly on his/her personal mobile number between 6:00 pm and 8:00 pm on all School Working Days. Parents can WhatsApp, SMS or email the Principal at any time and can expect a response within 24 hours . School Open House will be held for Half Yearly and Final Exams. Parents and Students' interest is paramount; in case of any emergency the above norms can be waived off as per the Principal's discretion. School Email Communications The School sends frequent emails to students on their official email ID. The following can be expected through Email: Majority of the information and notifications are sent through email. Daily Home Work is sent through email. Question Papers for CWA, OTT, SA (Examinations). Report Card. Outsourced vendor assessments. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Use of an Almanac
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Starting at LMS › Use of an Almanac
body: ← Starting at LMS Use of an Almanac An Almanac aims at establishing an invaluable link between Teachers, Students and Parents. It is intended to serve multiple purposes: As an all-in-one ready reckoner and planner for students. As a means of communication between Parents and the School. To keep Parents informed about their child's progress at School. To keep Parents informed with respect to assignments for home. To serve as a handy reference with regard to the student's schedule. To be used for convenient, day-to-day correspondence (related to Academics and Conduct) between Parents and the School. Student's Role Almanac must be brought to School daily. Must read all information in the Almanac and abide by it. Must update the Home Assignment daily. Performance Report should be updated simultaneously. Students of Class VI & above must give timely feedback on their teachers. Parent's Role Review the current page daily and must countersign. Check & sign Communication Record on page 4 daily. Check & sign Performance Reports at the end of the Almanac regularly. Use Almanac as the primary source of Communication with the Teacher. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Examination & CWA Rules
headings: Rules of Examination & CWA (Class Work Assessment)
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Examination & CWA Rules
body: ← Tips for Academic Life Rules of Examination & CWA (Class Work Assessment) Academic Year: The academic year will be divided into two semesters from March to February . Class Work Assessment will be conducted regularly. Report cards will be given to the parents at the end of the session. Rules of Examination & CWA Attendance is compulsory for all tests and examinations held during the year, failing which the student will be given a zero . No consideration will be given to absentees, except on medical grounds, at the sole discretion of the Head of the School. It is the responsibility of both students and their parents to ensure that they do not miss any Written Tests or Examinations. A minimum attendance record of 90% of the total number of working days is compulsory. Even on medical grounds, a child remaining absent for more than 20% of the total number of working days in each term, may not be allowed to give examination. There is no provision for re-testing students, who remain absent on the day of the examination, or those who have failed in one or more subjects. Students reporting late for examinations will not be given any extra time for answering the question papers. Students found using any unfair means during any of the examinations, will be given zero in that subject. A repeat of such cheating in the future will result in the prompt issue of a Transfer Certificate, without notice. The answer scripts of the examinations will be distributed to students after evaluation. Errors in evaluation — such as mistakes in totalling, unmarked answers etc. — must be brought to the notice of the concerned Teachers instantly . Delaying this, may result in ignoring the same while preparing the final result. Requests for the issue of Progress Cards before the declaration of results will not be entertained. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Extra Curricular Activities
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Extra Curricular Activities
body: ← Tips for Academic Life Role of Extra Curricular Activities Extra Curricular Activities and sports at LMS are as important as the Education itself . Extra Curricular Activities, Sports & Competitions ensure a holistically developed child. The Activities, Sports & Competitions are essential because they help students attain essential 21st Century Skills : Critical Thinking & Problem Solving Creativity & Innovation Collaboration Communication, Information & Media Literacy Cross-Cultural Understanding Computing & ICT Literacy They make the students confident and self-reliant. A lot of the activities are designed to help students get acquainted with possible skills, hobbies and interests. A number of competitions at School are derived from interview and evaluation centres, laying the foundation for competitive exams like UPSC, SSB, IBPS, CAT, SSC, etc. These also help students explore non-academic career options. How Competitions are Organised School Teachers mentor students about various types of extra curricular activities. A competition is usually held on a Tuesday at the Class level , where teachers give feedback and select the top 3–5 students for the School Level Competition. Students have a day to improve on the feedback. A School Level Competition is then held on a Thursday . LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Negative Impact of Private Tuition
headings: Negative Impact of Private Tuition Classes
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Private Tuition
body: ← Tips for Academic Life Negative Impact of Private Tuition Classes Tuition Classes in most cases are like slow poison for the School Students. The tuition classes can destroy a student's Critical Thinking & Problem Solving Powers . The Students also become dependent on other people for their own work. Tuition Classes either replace the Self-Study time or the Play time . Reduction in either of these have been known to have a negative impact on the development of the student. The Tuition Classes also take a toll on a student's family time . They have also been known to reduce the creativity of a student. In the senior classes, most students are known to acquire all sorts of bad & negative habits during the travel to the tuition classes. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Reading Habits
headings: Reading – The Most Precious Gift for Your Child
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Reading Habits
body: ← Tips for Academic Life Reading Habits & Its Nuances Reading – The Most Precious Gift for Your Child Reading is the most important skill children ever learn in School and one best strengthened by the untiring efforts of enthusiastic parents. Learning to read takes time and effort. Reading is not something children can just pick up — children need encouragement to work at it and to keep trying if they get frustrated. Reading is the doorway to a lifetime of learning. Children learn the nuts and bolts at School, but they still need the special one-to-one attention only parents can provide. Reading together must be a top priority in your daily agenda. Reading Tips Start today. The earlier, the better. Use the VAKT method : Visual, Audio, Kinaesthetic and Tactile — multi-sensory teaching techniques using all five sensory organs. Run your finger below the words as you read aloud. Show how you begin reading from the top and work down. Point out repetitive words. Ask children to spot these words. Talk about pictures. Unfamiliar words can be figured out by looking at pictures. Alternate story-reading sessions with days when your child narrates the story back to you. Beyond Books Children should have opportunities to go places and do different things. The more they experience, the easier it is to read because of new ideas and vocabulary they are exposed to. Get children involved in everyday reading — directions, grocery lists, recipes, labels, instructions, road signs. Subscribe to children's newspapers. The ability to listen and not merely to hear is a prerequisite to developing holistically. Limit gadgets during the initial 3 planes of Education. Encourage children to pen down their thoughts. Keep pencil crayons and paper handy. Approach to Reading A balanced reading programme should employ phonics — teaching children how to sound out words. The four key language skills to develop: Listening Skill Speaking Skill Reading Skill Writing Skill LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Role of Homework
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Tips for Academic Life › Role of Homework
body: ← Tips for Academic Life Role of Homework Homework is a constructive tool in the teaching/learning process, geared to the needs and abilities of students, for the reinforcement of matter learnt in School. It is also intended to help students develop independent study habits . Student Responsibilities Students should try and do their homework entirely on their own, without the help of their parents. They should develop a regular homework routine, keep their parents informed with respect to homework assignments and show them the finished work. Students should: Ensure that they are prepared with whatever stationery is needed for homework tasks. Give their parents sufficient notice, if any extra materials are needed for projects and other such home assignments, so that these can be acquired without inconvenience. Create, with the help of their parents, an appropriate environment at home for quiet study without disruption. Earmark a regular block of time for homework. If no homework is assigned they should maintain the routine, by reviewing class work or reading a book during the time assigned for homework. Ensure that their homework assignments are completed in time and that all supporting books and materials are carried back to the School. Seek assistance from their parents only when specifically required, as per instructions from their teacher. Ask their parents, on a daily basis, to review their homework tasks, show them the completed work and ensure that they sign the Almanac. Seek their parents' help to list the things they did not understand, so that they can share these with their teacher at School, the next day. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Absence from School
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Transport, Attendance & Timing › Absence from School
body: ← Transport, Attendance & Timing Absence from School A student, returning to School after suffering an infectious or contagious disease, should produce a doctor's fitness certificate permitting her/him to attend School. Parents will be promptly informed in the event of serious illness or emergencies and may be requested to come to School to take their ward home if the case is serious enough to merit their attention. Repeated absence without leave or unexplained absence for more than six consecutive days renders the student liable to have her/his name struck off the rolls. Re-admission may only be granted after payment of a re-admission fee. All students are expected to attend School on the opening day immediately following the vacations. Absence for more than three days after the vacations end, will attract strict disciplinary action . It is compulsory for students to complete 90% attendance (80% in case of medical reasons) in each term in order to be eligible to take the end-of-the-term examination. This stipulation can only be relaxed on genuine medical grounds, at the sole discretion of the Head of the School. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document

title: Transport Facility
breadcrumb: Home › Student Code of Conduct › Transport, Attendance & Timing › Transport Facility
body: ← Transport, Attendance & Timing Regarding Transport Facility (Conveyance) Students are expected to follow the given norms: Conveyance once availed cannot be terminated for the current year. Buses will not wait for late comers. No student should travel standing on the footboard. Students must not move around in the bus when it is in motion. No student should come near the entry door of the bus until it comes to complete halt. The front door of the bus is the only authorised entrance and exit. Boarding and alighting from buses should be done in silence and in an orderly manner. Students must make sure that the aisle of the bus is clear, and that school bags and other belongings are placed properly. Students must keep all parts of their body inside the bus at all times. They should not put their hands out even for waving. No object should be thrown inside or outside the bus. The consumption of eatables and drinks is not permitted in the bus. Unruly behaviour like shrieking and shouting is strictly prohibited; courteous behaviour is expected at all times. The driver's attention must not be distracted for any reason. The drivers are authorised to stop buses at designated stops only , unless otherwise directed by the bus teacher. The bus teacher and the monitor on duty are responsible for maintaining discipline in the bus. Any serious offence will be reported to the Head of School immediately. Parents/guardians of students who do not avail the School's transport should present themselves at the School gate 10 minutes before the start/dispersal of the School to drop/collect their wards. LMCS Bye Laws 2026 — La Montessori Chain of Schools — Confidential Internal Document
